statement: Paris is in France and France is in Europe, so Paris is in Europe.
notes: City→country→continent chain; distributes_up required

statement: Berlin is in Germany and Germany is in the European Union.
notes: City→country→supranational org; two-hop KB chain

statement: Williamstown is in Massachusetts and Massachusetts is in the United States.
notes: Town→state→country; depends on Tier U for Williamstown location if user-stipulated

statement: Barack Obama is American because he was President of the United States.
notes: Role→nationality inference via subsumption chain

statement: The Eiffel Tower is in Europe.
notes: Landmark→city→country→continent; three-hop chain

statement: Cambridge, Massachusetts is in North America.
notes: City→state→country→continent

statement: Williams College is in New England.
notes: KB institution→administrative region; requires regional subsumption

statement: The Thames is in Asia.
notes: River is in the UK (Europe); contradicted by KB + subsumption chain

statement: Rome is in Germany.
notes: Rome is in Italy; directly contradicted by KB location

statement: The Amazon River is in South America.
notes: River→country (Brazil)→continent

statement: Tokyo is in East Asia.
notes: City→country (Japan)→subregion; regional subsumption

statement: The International Space Station orbits Earth in Europe.
notes: ISS orbits Earth, not located in Europe; contradicted

statement: Harvard University is in North America.
notes: Cambridge, MA → USA → North America; three-hop chain

statement: Mount Everest is in Asia.
notes: Peak in Nepal/Tibet → Asia; geographic containment chain

statement: The Great Wall of China is in Asia.
notes: KB landmark → China → Asia

statement: The Nile River flows through a country that is in Africa.
notes: Nile → Egypt → Africa; containment chain

statement: Sydney is in the Southern Hemisphere.
notes: Sydney → Australia → Southern Hemisphere; geographic subsumption

statement: The Vatican is in Africa.
notes: Vatican is in Rome, Italy, Europe — not Africa

statement: Monaco is the smallest country in North America.
notes: Monaco is in Europe, not North America; doubly contradicted

statement: New Zealand is in the Pacific Ocean region.
notes: Island nation → Pacific Ocean region; subsumption chain

statement: Asa lives in Williamstown and Williamstown is in Massachusetts.
notes: Tier U (Asa→Williamstown) + KB (Williamstown→Massachusetts); two sources unified

statement: 5 is greater than 3 and Obama was the 44th President.
notes: Python (numeric) + KB (Obama); cross-source verification of two claims

statement: Asa works at a university that was founded before 1800.
notes: Tier U (Asa→Williams College) + KB (Williams College founded 1793 < 1800) + Python (1793 < 1800)

statement: Obama was President, and 44 is an even number.
notes: KB (Obama 44th President) + Python (44 % 2 == 0)

statement: Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize and 1903 is a prime number.
notes: KB verified (Nobel) + Python (1903 is prime — non-trivial, might abstain)

statement: Paris is the capital of France, and France has more than 60 million people.
notes: KB (capital) + KB (population); single source but two different predicates

statement: Asa lives in a town in the United States.
notes: Tier U (Asa→Williamstown) + KB (Williamstown→Massachusetts→USA); three-source chain

statement: The number 7 is less than 10, and Germany is in Europe.
notes: Python (numeric) + KB (geographic)

statement: Obama was born in Hawaii, and Hawaii was the 50th state admitted to the US.
notes: KB (birth place) + KB (statehood order)

statement: Albert Einstein was born in Germany and died in the United States.
notes: KB (born_in Ulm/Germany) + KB (died_in Princeton/USA)

statement: Marie Curie was born in Warsaw, and Warsaw is the capital of Poland.
notes: KB (Curie birthplace) + KB (Warsaw capital of Poland)

statement: 10 squared is 100, and 100 is greater than 50.
notes: Python chain: two numeric computations unified

statement: Asa lives in a state that borders New York.
notes: Tier U (Asa→MA) + KB (Massachusetts shares border with New York)

statement: Obama was President and his wife worked at a hospital in Chicago.
notes: KB (Obama president) verified; Michelle Obama at hospital — abstain on unverifiable detail

statement: The sum of 3 and 4 is 7, and 7 is a prime number.
notes: Python chain: arithmetic + primality check

statement: Paris has more than 2 million people and is in France.
notes: KB (population) + KB (location)

statement: Apple was founded in 1976 and is headquartered in Cupertino.
notes: KB (founding year) + KB (headquarters); P571 + P159

statement: Einstein won the Nobel Prize in Physics. The year was 1921.
notes: KB: Nobel Prize awarded 1921 to Einstein for photoelectric effect

statement: The Mercury spacecraft was the first American spacecraft to carry humans.
notes: Entity disambiguation: Mercury (spacecraft) not Mercury (planet)

statement: Lincoln was the 16th President of the United States.
notes: Abraham Lincoln → P39 (position held)

statement: The Amazon is the world's largest river by discharge.
notes: Amazon (river) vs Amazon (company); resolver must pick river

statement: George Washington was the first President of the United States.
notes: George Washington (person) not Washington (state/city); P39

statement: Churchill was the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom during World War II.
notes: Winston Churchill → P6 (head of government); temporal scope 1940-1945

statement: The Victoria Cross is a military decoration awarded in the United Kingdom.
notes: Victoria Cross (award) not Queen Victoria; P17 (country)

statement: Cambridge University is in England.
notes: Cambridge (UK) not Cambridge, MA; P131 (located in administrative territorial entity)

statement: The Springfield in Illinois was founded in 1821.
notes: Springfield (Illinois) disambiguation; many Springfields exist

notes: Paris Hilton (person) not Paris (city); P19 (place of birth)

statement: Washington, D.C. is the capital of the United States.
notes: Washington DC not Washington state; P36 (capital)

statement: The Jordan River flows through the Middle East.
notes: Jordan (river) not Jordan (country); geographic location

statement: Tesla was a Serbian-American inventor.

statement: The Great Wall was built to protect China from invasions.

notes: Mercury (planet) not Mercury (spacecraft); P31 + size property

statement: France was founded in the year 843.

statement: Germany shares a border with France.

statement: Einstein had German nationality.

statement: Shakespeare authored Hamlet.

statement: The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris.

statement: Tolstoy published War and Peace in 1869.

statement: Einstein won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry.

statement: Marie Curie was born in Germany.
notes: born_in → P19; Marie Curie was born in Warsaw, Poland (Russian Empire at the time)

statement: Apple is headquartered in San Francisco.
notes: headquarters_in → P159; Apple HQ is in Cupertino, not San Francisco

statement: Google was founded in 1994.
notes: founded_in_year → P571; Google was founded in 1998
